title: Forge admitted manifest (manifests/admitted.json)
description: Authoritative, REVERSIBLE record of every resource ACTIVATED into the active library by `forge catalog admit` (catalog-until-admitted, ADR-0017). Each entry is one admission: admit copied the resource's bytes from its source resource root to the canonical library target (kind+id -> componentCandidates) and appended a provenance record here so `forge catalog revoke` can delete the copied target and restore a replaced original. Owned by manager/catalog.mjs (admit/revoke). admit copies BYTES ONLY and NEVER executes fetched code (ADR-0017 §security). This manifest is NOT consumed by the composition validator (validate-manifests only checks modules.json/profiles.json); it is the catalog operator's own reversible state.

schema.description: On-disk schema tag for this manifest.

_note.description: Free-form authoring note; ignored by the catalog operator.

admitted.description: One record per ACTIVATED resource, keyed (uniquely) by targetPath — one library path has at most one active admission.

uid.description: Registry artifact uid "<kind>:<id>" of the admitted resource.

sourceId.description: The federated source id the bytes were admitted from (manifests/sources.json#sources[].id). Empty string for a local/library-origin admission.

repoUrl.description: Clone URL (git) or filesystem path (local) of the source the bytes came from, copied from the source provenance at admit time. May be empty when unknown.

ref.description: Branch/tag/commit the source tracked at admit time (provenance ref). May be empty.

commit.description: Exact upstream commit the admitted bytes were synced from (.forge/sources.lock pin). May be empty when the source was unpinned.

kind.description: The SINGULAR registry kind of the admitted resource (agent|skill|command|rule|bundle|workflow|mcp). Used by revoke to know whether the target is a file or a skill directory.

targetPath.description: Repo-relative (POSIX) path of the activated resource under the FORGE library root (the canonical componentCandidates target). For a skill this is the skills/<id>/SKILL.md file; revoke removes its containing skill dir.

replaced.description: Present ONLY when the activation OVERWROTE an existing library resource (a REPLACE, gated by a T2 --override). Holds the original bytes so revoke can restore them.

path.description: Repo-relative (POSIX) path of the replaced resource (equals targetPath).

backupB64.description: Base64 of the original bytes. For a file: base64 of the file. For a skill dir: base64 of a JSON map { "<relpath>": "<base64-bytes>" } of every file under the dir, so revoke can rebuild the original tree.
